facility: University of Maryland Medical Center — Emergency Radiology
indication: Recurrent dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain after a 3-day peri-procedural anticoagulation hold; evaluate for recurrent/progressed PE.
technique: CT pulmonary angiography, pulmonary arterial phase, with reformats.
findings: Pulmonary emboli are now present in BOTH MAIN pulmonary arteries with extension into lobar branches bilaterally — PROGRESSED compared with the segmental right-lower-lobe embolus on the 7/6 CTPA. The RV:LV ratio is increased (>1.0) with reflux of contrast into the IVC/hepatic veins, suggesting right-heart strain. No frank pulmonary infarction. The partially imaged liver lesion is again noted, grossly unchanged.
impression:
1. PROGRESSED, now BILATERAL pulmonary embolism with CT signs of right-ventricular strain (RV:LV >1.0) — intermediate-risk.
2. Marked interval progression compared with the 7/6 study, in the setting of the recent anticoagulation interruption.
comparison: 7/6/2027 CTPA showed only a segmental right-lower-lobe embolus with a normal RV:LV ratio. Significant interval progression.
